$schema_comment: ai-memory v0.7.0 NSA CSI capability inventory. Source-of-truth artefact produced by Task I of the NSA CSI audit (issue #1153). Every row is codegraph + grep verified.

test_counts.note: Authoritative test-attribute count. Earlier '13k+' claims are not grep-verifiable and have been retired from v0.7.0 messaging per issue #1153 §3 correction.

mcp_tool_counts.authority_source: src/profile.rs — profile_core_has_seven_tools (line 833), profile_graph_has_eighteen_tools (849), profile_admin_has_twenty_tools (860), profile_power_has_sixteen_tools (870), profile_full_has_seventy_three_tools (898)

memory_portability_spec.note: Originating brief referenced 'v1.1' — authoritative is v1 per the document's own status line.

capabilities.display_name: Form 4 fact-provenance (citations + source URI + byte-range span)
capabilities.factual_description: Every memory row carries fact-grain Citation envelopes (uri, accessed_at, optional hash, optional span), a first-class source_uri pointer (uri:/doc:/file: schemes), and an optional byte-range source_span ({start, end}) for atom-grain provenance. The substrate validates every citation, URI, and span at the write boundary via the RequestValidator surface (#966).
capabilities.originating_brief_correction: Originating brief referenced 'atom_span' — actual field name is 'source_span'. The Citation envelope doc-comment at line 601 cross-references 'source_span' consistently.

capabilities.schema_anchor: v30 (memory_kind column added to memories table)
capabilities.factual_description: Every memory carries a typed MemoryKind discriminator: Observation, Reflection, Persona, Concept, Entity, Claim, Relation, Event, Conversation, Decision. Each variant has explicit semantics documented in-source. Allows downstream consumers to filter to propositional commits (Claim), captured dialogue (Conversation), reflective summaries (Reflection), etc.
capabilities.verified_by_codegraph: codegraph_node MemoryKind at src/models/memory.rs:38 — 10 variants confirmed

capabilities.display_name: Form 7 + L1-6 agent-EXTERNAL governance rules engine
capabilities.factual_description: The substrate consults the operator-signed AgentAction rules engine on every write that proposes an agent-EXTERNAL action. Rules are Ed25519-signed by the operator and stored as substrate rows. Default posture flipped to enforce in v0.7.0 (#1054 fail-CLOSED): writes that fail rule consultation are refused unless the operator opts in to the fail-OPEN escape hatch.

Every signed governance rule is hashed over a canonical byte representation that explicitly includes the `enabled` flag (preventing enable-after-sign tampering) and explicitly excludes the signature itself plus the attest_level (preventing self-referential signature loops). Two regression tests pin the canonical-bytes invariant.

Every append to the signed_events audit table carries a SHA-256 prev_hash referencing the prior row's canonical bytes plus a monotonic sequence counter. Tampering with row N's content breaks row N+1's prev_hash verification; tampering with sequence breaks the contiguity check. The chain is tamper-evident even when individual row signatures are valid. CLI verifier ai-memory verify-signed-events-chain walks the chain backward and emits a structured report; non-zero exit on chain break.
capabilities.verified_by_codegraph: codegraph_search prev_hash sequence signed_events → confirmed at src/signed_events.rs:100-119

verified_at_paths: tests/http_if_match_concurrency.rs:128 (HTTP PUT with matching If-Match succeeds)
tests/http_if_match_concurrency.rs:149 (HTTP PUT with stale If-Match returns 409)
tests/http_if_match_concurrency.rs:184 (PUT without If-Match preserves legacy last-write-wins)
tests/http_if_match_concurrency.rs:219 (unparseable If-Match falls through to legacy)
capabilities.schema_anchor: v45 (memories.version BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 column added)
capabilities.factual_description: Every memory row carries a monotonic version counter bumped on every storage update. Concurrent updates passing expected_version race exactly one winner; the loser receives a typed 409 CONFLICT envelope naming the current stored version. HTTP wire surface honors If-Match header in both bare and ETag-quoted forms; missing If-Match preserves last-write-wins for backwards compat.

capabilities.schema_anchor: v38 (memories.source_uri TEXT NULL column + idx_memories_source_uri partial index)
capabilities.factual_description: Every memory may carry a first-class URI pointer to its source artefact. Accepted schemes: uri: (HTTP URL), doc: (substrate doc id), file: (filesystem path). Validator surface lives at crate::validate::validate_source_uri. Partial index idx_memories_source_uri (v38) covers the predicate for fast source-side query.

capabilities.display_name: Seven-gap Gap 3: causal recall observation tracking
capabilities.factual_description: Every memory_recall invocation writes a recall_observations row keyed by a UUIDv4 recall_id, recording which candidates were considered, scored, and surfaced. The memory_recall_observations MCP tool queries the observation log by recall_id, allowing forensic reconstruction of any recall decision against any historical query.

Memories carry a typed ConfidenceTier enum surfaced via the capabilities v3 envelope. Downstream consumers (LLM agents, application logic, audit reviewers) may filter by confidence tier when applying provenance gates.

When a memory supersedes a prior version, the superseded row is archived into archived_memories with archive_reason='superseded' rather than overwritten. The reciprocal forward pointer preserves the audit chain; archived rows remain queryable for forensic review.

verified_at_paths: src/mcp/tools/search.rs:120 (db::search_with_source_uri for combined FTS + source filter)
capabilities.factual_description: The memory_search MCP tool accepts a source_uri parameter validated via validate_source_uri at the wire boundary. When supplied with an empty query, routes to db::list_by_source_uri (index-only fast path); when combined with FTS query, routes to db::search_with_source_uri. The partial index idx_memories_source_uri covers the predicate.
capabilities.note: Pre-audit (issue #1153 §2) flagged this as partial-verify; full deep-verify in Task I confirms shipped.

verified_at_paths: src/mcp/tools/recall.rs:284 (decorate_memory function with verbose_provenance: bool argument)
capabilities.factual_description: The recall pipeline accepts an opt-in verbose_provenance flag that adds Form 4/5/6 provenance signals to every recalled memory envelope. When false (default), the response collapses to the legacy v0.6.x wire shape preserving backwards compatibility. When true, citations, source_uri, source_span, ConfidenceTier, and memory_kind are all surfaced in the response envelope.

The capabilities envelope at v0.7.0 carries schema_version=3 with three application blocks (Form 4 provenance signals, Form 5 confidence calibration, Form 6 typed memory-kind). Clients may pin to v1 or v2 via accept= on memory_capabilities MCP or Accept-Capabilities HTTP header. Backwards compat preserved.

The federation sync daemon refuses to start without mTLS unless an explicit insecure flag is set. Peer certificates are validated against an operator-supplied allowlist; an empty allowlist refuses every peer (defense-in-depth against operator misconfiguration). Missing cert files raise a typed error at config-load time.

Every agent may carry an Ed25519 keypair generated via ai-memory identity generate. Outbound link writes sign canonical-CBOR-encoded link content; inbound verification checks signatures against the observed_by claim. attest_level enum: unsigned / self_signed / peer_attested. Hardware-backed key storage (TPM/HSM/Secure Enclave) is out of OSS scope and lives in the AgenticMem commercial layer.

Every memory carries a namespace string validated against a strict allowlist regex (max 128 bytes, no slashes/spaces/nulls). Each namespace may carry a standard policy memory pointer (Batman Form 2 + Form 6 enabled by #800). Multi-tenant isolation is enforced at the storage layer via the namespace foreign key on every read path.

capabilities.schema_anchor: v48 (federation_push_dlq table added to sqlite + postgres)
capabilities.factual_description: When a federation push to a peer fails (network error, peer rejection, signature failure), the push is enqueued to the federation_push_dlq table with a retry counter. A background replay loop drains the DLQ with exponential backoff up to MAX_REPLAY_ATTEMPTS; depth is exported as a Prometheus gauge for operator monitoring.

verified_at_paths: src/hooks/mod.rs (decision contract: Allow / Modify / Deny / AskUser)
capabilities.factual_description: The substrate fires hook events at every memory lifecycle transition: 20 baseline events (PreStore/PostStore/PreRecall/PostRecall/PreSearch/PostSearch/PreDelete/PostDelete/PrePromote/PostPromote/PreLink/PostLink/PreConsolidate/PostConsolidate/PreGovernanceDecision/PostGovernanceDecision/OnIndexEviction/PreArchive/PreTranscriptStore/PostTranscriptStore) plus 5 v0.7.0 additions (PreRecallExpand for G10 query expansion, PreReflect + PostReflect for recursive-learning Task 6/8, PreCompaction + OnCompactionRollback for L1-7 compaction). Hooks fire only when ~/.config/ai-memory/hooks.toml exists and registers them. Decision contract supports veto (Deny), modification (Modify), and human-approval gating (AskUser).
capabilities.verified_by_codegraph: codegraph_node HookEvent at src/hooks/events.rs:73 — 25 variants confirmed

capabilities.factual_description: Federation convergence is tested under chaos conditions in tests/federation_scale.rs. The p99 latency percentile is pinned at 0.99 (test constant). A campaign-artefact convergence threshold (operator-side documentation referenced as ≥0.995 in the originating brief) is tracked under docs/v0.7.0/test-campaign-* but is NOT a code-side constant — Task I HONEST POSTURE: this threshold lives in operator campaign documentation, not in test code. The code-side guarantee is the p99 latency budget.
capabilities.honesty_note: Originating brief's ≥0.995 figure is campaign-artefact-tracked, not code-pinned. Procurement-grade evidence relies on the documented A2A chaos campaign results in docs/v0.7.0/test-campaign-*, not a hardcoded threshold.

verified_at_paths: tests/token_budget_guard.rs:75 (TRIMMED_FULL_PROFILE_CEILING_TOKENS for the bare wire ceiling)
capabilities.factual_description: The MCP wire surface is bounded by cl100k_base token ceilings. The trimmed bare-wire ceiling holds tools/list under the C5 budget (the strip_docs_from_tools wire trimmer drops long-form natural-language strings, doc fields, and nested description strings). The verbose-surface ceiling applies when callers explicitly request memory_capabilities verbose drilldown. Deterministic tokenization pinned by the cl100k_tokenizer_is_deterministic regression test.

The Memory Portability Spec defines an export envelope any ai-memory deployment can produce and any ai-memory deployment can ingest without data loss. Specification version v1 (Draft v1, originally v0.6.3.1; carried forward at v0.7.0). Multi-implementation interop is a v2.0 deliverable per ROADMAP §7.6.
capabilities.originating_brief_correction: Originating brief referenced v1.1 — authoritative version stamp is v1 per the document's own header.

capabilities.factual_description: Every wire-entry layer (HTTP handlers, MCP tools, CLI subcommands) routes DTO-bundling validation through pub struct RequestValidator — the canonical fluent surface. Pre-#966 the same validate_id + validate_namespace + validate_agent_id + ... chains were duplicated across 87 HTTP routes, 73 MCP tools, and 58 CLI subcommands; adding a new cross-field invariant required three audited per-surface edits. Post-#966 a single struct method covers all three surfaces with the typed ValidationError { field, reason } envelope. This is the substrate's primary defense against the NSA-enumerated tool-parameter-injection class.

verified_at_paths: src/subscriptions.rs:1070-1100 (SSRF guard with DNS resolution + IP allowlist, #1053 fail-CLOSED)
capabilities.factual_description: DoS defense is multi-layer: (1) K8 per-agent quota with memory_quota_status MCP tool + /api/v1/quota/status HTTP endpoint surfaces day-by-day storage and rate budgets to operators. (2) cl100k_base token budget guards bound the MCP wire surface (tools/list trimmed under the C5 budget; verbose drilldown bounded at VERBOSE_FULL_PROFILE_CEILING_TOKENS). (3) HMAC-mandatory webhook dispatch under R3-S1.HMAC fails-CLOSED — daemon refuses to send unsigned subscriptions. (4) SSRF guard validates webhook target URLs via DNS resolution against an IP allowlist; fails-CLOSED on DNS failure under AI_MEMORY_SSRF_GUARD_ALLOW_DNS_FAIL. (5) Federation push DLQ caps retry storms at MAX_REPLAY_ATTEMPTS. (6) HTTP body size capped at 2 MB via Axum DefaultBodyLimit. (7) Federation nonce-replay defense prevents amplification via captured-signature replay.
capabilities.honesty_note: DoS hardening is perpetual — no substrate can fully defend against legitimate-but-expensive queries without operator-tuned quotas. The seven layers above raise the cost of attack but do not eliminate the threat class.

capabilities.factual_description: The substrate ships three substrate-native inspection subcommands that do NOT use Anthropic's separate MCP-Inspector toolchain (the surface CVE-2025-49596 targeted). ai-memory verify-reflection-chain walks the reflects_on edge graph backward from a memory id and verifies every Ed25519 signature. ai-memory verify-signed-events-chain walks the V-4 cross-row hash chain (prev_hash + sequence) on the signed_events audit table and reports tampering with non-zero exit. ai-memory verify-forensic-bundle re-hashes every file in a procurement-grade evidence bundle, checks the manifest signature, and re-verifies every edge signature against the bundled observed_by public key. Substrate operators using these verifiers are not vulnerable to CVE-2025-49596 (which was in the separate MCP-Inspector toolchain, not the ai-memory substrate).

capabilities.display_name: MCP initialize handshake clientInfo capture + daemon serverInfo Ed25519 signing
verified_at_paths: src/mcp/mod.rs:1607-1611 (clientInfo capture at initialize handshake — v0.7.0 baseline)
src/mcp/server_identity.rs (daemon Ed25519 serverInfo signing — #1154 net-new module)
src/mcp/mod.rs initialize handler (constructs + signs ai_memory_identity block; omits when no keypair)
src/governance/audit.rs:558 (load_daemon_signing_key — threaded into handle_request as active_keypair)

tests/mcp_initialize_server_signing.rs (27 integration tests: happy path, no-keypair fallback, backwards compat, tampering rejection, malformed-input rejection, cross-rotation TOFU detection, determinism, performance, schema-version drift)
capabilities.factual_description: Composite defense, both halves shipped at v0.7.x: (1) the substrate captures clientInfo.name from the MCP initialize handshake and threads it through every downstream operation for per-row provenance attribution; (2) the substrate publishes a daemon-Ed25519-signed ai_memory_identity block on every MCP initialize response so clients can implement Trust On First Use (TOFU) pinning of the daemon fingerprint. The ai_memory_identity block carries schema_version + daemon_id + public_key + signed_at + Ed25519 signature. Canonical-bytes discipline mirrors the existing governance-rule signing pattern at src/governance/rules_store.rs:541. Block is OMITTED when daemon has no keypair on disk, preserving the v0.7.0 'continuing unsigned' posture for backwards compat. v0.6.4 / v0.7.0 clients ignore the additive field per MCP / JSON-RPC convention.

Operators may build with --features sqlcipher to enable SQLite-level AES-256 encryption at rest. When AI_MEMORY_ENCRYPT_AT_REST=true, every DB open path requires an AI_MEMORY_DB_PASSPHRASE (or --db-passphrase-file with mode 0400 strict permission check per #1055). Plain-sqlite opens are refused under this posture. The passphrase-file lax-permission escape hatch (AI_MEMORY_PASSPHRASE_FILE_ALLOW_LAX_PERMS) defaults to false, requiring 0400-or-tighter on first start.
capabilities.honesty_note: Switching the flag on against an existing plain DB does NOT encrypt it — operators must export → encrypted-init → import or use the sqlcipher CLI ATTACH … KEY recipe.

capabilities.factual_description: Evaluated on ICLR 2025 LongMemEval-S (500 questions, 6 categories). Pure FTS5 keyword tier achieves 97.0% R@5 in 2.2 seconds (232 q/s). LLM query expansion (smart tier) pushes to 97.8% R@5. Recall at depth 10 = 99.0%; recall at depth 20 = 99.8%. All inference runs locally — zero cloud API calls.

Field name atom_span → source_span (Memory.source_span at src/models/memory.rs:482)

nsa_csi_mcp_coverage_status.closure_milestone: #1154 daemon serverInfo Ed25519 signing at MCP initialize handshake — shipped in this PR with 47 dedicated tests (20 module + 27 integration); zero regression on existing MCP handshake tests (mcp_initialize_handshake_succeeds, 8 d4_*_initialize_round_trip harness coverage tests, test_mcp_initialize legacy integration test)

v0_7_x_followup_polish_items: Accept-Provenance HTTP header capability (#1155) — MCP wire default verbose_provenance=true ALREADY ships at v0.7.0 per src/mcp/tools/recall.rs:490; only the HTTP-side Accept-Provenance header is genuinely net-new. NOT a coverage gap, polish only.
Per-namespace rate-limit dimension extension (#1156) — defense-in-depth on the 7-layer DoS defense already shipping. NOT a coverage gap, polish only.
